briefdescription.para: The bit in the EMCY COB-ID specifying whether the EMCY exists and is valid.
The bit in the EMCY COB-ID specifying whether to use an 11-bit (0) or 29-bit (1) CAN-ID.

memberdef.definition: typedef void co_emcy_ind_t(co_emcy_t *emcy, co_unsigned8_t id, co_unsigned16_t eec, co_unsigned8_t er, co_unsigned8_t msef[5], void *data)
memberdef.argsstring: (co_emcy_t *emcy, co_unsigned8_t id, co_unsigned16_t eec, co_unsigned8_t er, co_unsigned8_t msef[5], void *data)
memberdef.name: co_emcy_ind_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen EMCY indication function, invoked when an EMCY message is received.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an EMCY consumer service.
the node-ID of the producer.
the emergency error code.
the error register.
the manufacturer-specific error code.
a pointer to user-specified data.

briefdescription.para: Returns the alignment (in bytes) of the co_emcy_t structure.
Returns the size (in bytes) of the co_emcy_t structure.
Creates a new CANopen EMCY producer/consumer service.

detaileddescription.para: The service is stopped after creation and SHOULD be started with co_emcy_start() before use.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network.
a pointer to a CANopen device.

simplesect.para: a pointer to a new EMCY service, or NULL on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Destroys a CANopen EMCY producer/consumer service.
Starts an EMCY service.
Stops an EMCY service.
Returns true if the specified EMCY service is stopped, and false if not.
Returns a pointer to the allocator used to allocate an EMCY producer/consumer service.
Returns a pointer to the CAN network of an EMCY producer/consumer service.
Returns a pointer to the CANopen device of an EMCY producer/consumer service.

memberdef.argsstring: (co_emcy_t *emcy, co_unsigned16_t eec, co_unsigned8_t er, const co_unsigned8_t msef[5])
memberdef.name: co_emcy_push

briefdescription.para: Pushes a CANopen EMCY message to the stack and broadcasts it if the EMCY producer service is active.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an EMCY producer service.
the emergency error code.
the error register.
the manufacturer-specific error code (can be NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Pops the most recent CANopen EMCY message from the stack and broadcasts an 'error reset' message if the EMCY producer service is active.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an EMCY producer service.
the address at which to store the emergency error code (can be NULL).
the address at which to store the error register (can be NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Retrieves, but does not pop, the most recent CANopen EMCY message from the stack.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an EMCY producer service.
the address at which to store the emergency error code (can be NULL).
the address at which to store the error register (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Clears the CANopen EMCY message stack and broadcasts the 'error reset/no error' message if the EMCY producer service is active.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked when a CANopen EMCY message is received.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an EMCY consumer service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when a CANopen EMCY message is received.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an EMCY consumer service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to func.

briefdescription.para: This header file is part of the CANopen library; it contains the emergency (EMCY) object declarations.

detaileddescription.para:
